extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR marked EVENEPOEL's attack on the final 11% climb, countered LAFAY's move, and soloed to victory with bonus seconds
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE's early pace-forcing attempted to isolate POGAČAR before final climb, forcing Quick-Step to commit tactically early.
EVENEPOEL's attack on final ramps immediately covered by POGAČAR wheel-to-wheel, revealing Quick-Step cannot distance him on steep gradients.
LAFAY's late move drew POGAČAR's counter rather than forcing teammates to chase, showing UAE isolation concerns were overblown.
SIMMONS prioritized KOM points over breakaway survival, showing green jersey disruption through selective mountain sprints works on undulating stages.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: marked every move personally without teammates, then soloed clear on 11% finale showing no weakness when isolated
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: unable to distance POGAČAR despite launching on steepest ramps where his TT power theoretically gives advantage
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: domestique role for EVENEPOEL rather than own ambitions, attempted early isolation tactics that failed
rider_signals.LAFAY Victor: positive: aggressive late move earned podium when others marked EVENEPOEL, showing value in attacking after favorites engage
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: positive: broke from established breakaway to steal KOM points, disrupting BAIS's jersey defense through selective targeting
rider_signals.BAIS Davide: negative: failed to make breakaway while wearing KOM jersey, lost it to SIMMONS who prioritized point collection
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: present in finale group but no acceleration attempted, box-ticking for GC rather than stage hunting
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: neutral: attempted move on finale but marked, showing presence without power to sustain attacks against POGAČAR
